integrity.description: Ensure the test results are from a genuine terminal execution and not faked.
integrity.visual_evidence: Test output should appear in a standard terminal emulator or IDE run tool window, complete with standard execution times and formatting, not manually typed into a text file.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually edited the Python files using an editor or IDE.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the code open in an IDE (like PyCharm) or a terminal-based text editor (like nano or vim), rather than just echoing text to the console.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not cheat by modifying the test files to force them to pass.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not have files from the 'tests/' directory open for editing to remove assertions or hardcode 'True' returns. Edits should be restricted to 'core.py' and 'validators.py'.
